name: Tucson Mini wordlist blocklist
description: STWL contains valid English fill that's still awkward for a warm Sunday mini — smush-words ('a' + verb), niche brand names, archaic forms that even a crossword regular finds annoying. Listed words are removed from the candidate pool entirely; the grid generator never proposes them. Add freely as bad fill turns up in generated puzzles.
notes_for_editor: Names like STACY are NOT blocked — the issue with the first-run STACY clue was the LLM inventing a 'Tucson dog-park name,' not the answer itself. The prompt change handles invented local color.
Crossword conventions like ASEA, ASTIR, ANEW are intentionally left in. They're not great fill but they're not what makes a Tucson reader frown — the smush-words and weird brand names are.
Spanish loanwords (ESOS, AGUA, ELOTE, etc.) are NOT blocked — they're a feature in a Tucson puzzle.
If you find a word here that you actually want back, just remove it from the blocklist.
